verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytoplasmic heat shock protein (Hsp70) family member. The NCBI summary explicitly states its functions are in the cytosol and organelles (ER/mitochondria via TIM23 complex interaction), with roles in protein folding, anti-aggregation, and ubiquitin-proteasome pathway. No membrane-anchoring domain, GPI anchor, or surface-trafficking mechanism is documented. The protein is soluble and compartmentalized intracellularly. While heat shock proteins can be transiently released under stress, HSPA1A lacks a stable mechanism to reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. No cell-surface therapeutic programs target HSPA1A on the cell surface, and the protein body does not possess its own mechanism for outer-leaflet presentation.
